title: Something Amazing That No One Predicted Just Happened in the Markets (Yes, AI Is at the Center of It)
author: Lee Samaha
description: GE Vernova surpassed GE Aerospace in market capitalization for the first time, driven by surging AI data center demand for its gas turbines. The HA turbine received over four times more orders in 2025 than in 2018, with both companies benefiting from strong management and technological advantages. GE Vernova raised full-year guidance due to booming orders, while GE Aerospace faces headwinds from the Iran conflict.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surpassed GE Aerospace in market cap for the first time; HA turbine orders increased 4x from 2018 to 2025; raised full-year revenue, earnings, and cash flow guidance; strong AI data center demand driving order book growth; excellent management positioning company for sustained growth.

Stock up 97% over three years with strong fundamentals and LEAP engine dominance on Boeing 737 MAX and Airbus A320neo; however, Iran conflict is constraining growth prospects and could lead to aircraft engine retirements if high oil prices persist; beat Q1 expectations but faces near-term headwinds.
Mentioned as operator of 737 MAX aircraft powered by CFM International's LEAP engine; no direct commentary on Boeing's performance, but benefits from GE Aerospace's engine supply relationship.

title: Iran Declares Strait Of Hormuz Open To All Vessels: Crude Plunges 14%, Airlines And Cruise Stocks Soar
author: Piero Cingari
description: Iran's Foreign Minister announced the Strait of Hormuz is fully open to all commercial vessels during the ceasefire, causing crude oil to plunge 14% to $81/barrel. Airlines and cruise lines surged as fuel costs declined, while energy and chemical companies fell sharply. The S&P 500 reached record highs with the Nasdaq 100 on its 13th consecutive gaining session.

title: Here's Why FTAI Aviation Shares Are Soaring Today
author: Lee Samaha
description: FTAI Aviation shares surged 10.35% following a U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement that led to declining oil prices. Lower energy costs benefit the company's two main business segments: aircraft engine servicing (higher fuel prices reduce flight demand) and FTAI Power's data center turbine conversion business (lower energy costs improve data center investment viability).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Lower oil prices from the U.S.-Iran ceasefire reduce jet fuel costs, encouraging more flight departures and increasing demand for aircraft engine servicing. Additionally, lower energy prices improve the viability of data center investments, benefiting FTAI Power's turbine conversion business. The stock gained 10.35% on this positive catalyst.

title: Centene, Paramount Skydance, And Ulta Beauty Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (March 9-March 13): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of March 9-13, 2026. Fair Isaac Corporation led losses with a 21.59% drop following a $1 billion senior notes offering announcement. Other major decliners included Centene (20.85%), Ulta Beauty (16.04%), Paramount Skydance (15.4%), and Thomson Reuters (13.97%). Declines were attributed to factors including disappointing earnings guidance, analyst downgrades, and broader market pressures across healthcare, beauty, media, and aviation sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 21.59% following announcement of a $1.0 billion senior notes offering, indicating significant capital raising needs and investor concern

title: FTAI Aviation Expands Relationship with Air France Through Transaction Focused on End-of-Life Fleet Strategy
author: Globe Newswire
description: FTAI Aviation has closed the acquisition of seven off-lease Airbus aircraft from Air France, including one A318-100, four A319-100, and two A321-200 aircraft. The transaction supports Air France's fleet modernization and provides FTAI with additional engine and module feedstock for its Maintenance, Repair and Exchange solutions, particularly CFM56 engines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully closed a strategic acquisition that expands its relationship with a major airline partner, secures additional engine feedstock for its growing Maintenance, Repair and Exchange business, and demonstrates its differentiated capabilities in asset value creation and fleet management solutions.

The company successfully closed a strategic acquisition that expands its relationship with a major airline partner, secures additional engine feedstock for its growing Maintenance, Repair and Exchange business, and demonstrates its differentiated capabilities in asset value creation and fleet management solutions.

The company successfully closed a strategic acquisition that expands its relationship with a major airline partner, secures additional engine feedstock for its growing Maintenance, Repair and Exchange business, and demonstrates its differentiated capabilities in asset value creation and fleet management solutions.

Air France benefits from this transaction as it supports the airline's continued modernization of its narrowbody fleet and provides a flexible, comprehensive fleet management solution through its partnership with FTAI.
